title: Longest Palindromic Subsequence Length
description:
Given a string s, find the length of the longest palindromic subsequence.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ |s| ≤ 1000
explanation:
Intuition
Let dp[i][j] be LPS length in s[i..j]. If s[i]==s[j], dp=2+dp[i+1][j-1]; else max of dp[i+1][j] and dp[i][j-1]. Fill for increasing lengths.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize dp[i][i]=1
2. Main Algorithm: For len from 2..n, fill i..j using recurrence
3. Return Result: Return dp[0][n-1]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^2)
- Space Complexity: O(n^2)

Key Insights
- Interval DP over substrings
- Reverse relation to LCS between s and reversed s

Common Pitfalls
- Be careful with i+1>j-1 base
- Indices must expand outward correctly

testCases.explanation: When lps_length(s="cbbd"), the expected result is 2
When lps_length(s="agbdba"), the expected result is 5

hints: Interval DP from shorter to longer
Else take max of excluding one end
